content:
Let $p$ and $q$ be any two propositions. Consider the following propositional statements.

$$ egin{aligned} & S_1: p ightarrow q, \quad S_2: eg p \wedge q, \quad S_3: eg p \vee q, \ & S_4: eg p \vee eg q, \end{aligned} $$

Where $\wedge$ denotes conjunction (AND operation), $\vee$ denotes disjunction (OR operation), and $ eg$ denotes negation

(NOT operation). Which one of the following options is correct?

(Note: $\equiv$ denotes logical equivalence)
explanation:
We know that

$$ \begin{aligned} & p \rightarrow q=\sim p V q \\ & =\sim q \rightarrow \sim p \end{aligned} $$

So $S_1$ and $S_3$ are equal.

Hence, the correct option is (A).
